summary:
**Claim**: The estimated dental age is 10 years.  
**Evidence**: Permanent first molars confirm age ≥6–7 years; visible canines (though unerupted) are typical at ages 8–9, supporting a developmental stage beyond early childhood. Absence of second molars or premolars indicates the individual has not yet reached the typical eruption window (10–12 years), ruling out older ages.  
**Answer**: B
